plan: Audit the existing image tools page, add a compact set of project-relevant offline browser image utilities, improve the page structure using the existing dark studio visual system, then verify build and production preview evidence.
actions: Read current route, layout, ImageTools implementation, shared canvas helpers, and visual component conventions.
Reworked src/pages/ImageTools.tsx into a six-tool offline image lab while preserving existing compose, stitch, and pixel tools.
Added format export/compression, transparent trim, and palette extraction panels using browser File, Canvas, Blob, and Clipboard APIs without backend calls.
Added src/index.css styles for the new hero, tool stats, sticky controls, preview stages, palette grid, and mobile fallbacks.
Fixed object URL cleanup so multi-batch image uploads do not revoke images still in state.

verification.summary: TypeScript project build and Vite production build completed successfully; only the pre-existing vendor-antd chunk-size warning remains.

verification.command: vite preview on http://127.0.0.1:4174/image-tools plus in-app browser DOM snapshot
verification.summary: Production preview rendered the ImageTools route with hero content, six tabs, and default format-export panel.
Browser console error check returned no error logs for the production preview route.
No em-dash or en-dash characters found in the changed user-facing tool page files.

errors: CodeGraph was unavailable because the workspace is not initialized; direct file inspection was used instead.
In-app browser screenshot capture timed out once, so DOM snapshot plus console log checks were used for runtime evidence.
selfReflection: The slice directly matches the broad goal: more project-relevant offline tools were added and the tool page now reads as an integrated local image lab rather than a small tab dump. The existing Ant Design plus emerald dark visual system was preserved, and motion was not downgraded.

selfAssessment.reason: A focused ImageTools expansion delivered meaningful offline utility and visible design improvement without disturbing routes or unrelated pages.

successSignalUpdates.text: The project contains more browser-local, project-relevant image tools presented in a coherent and usable tool page.

artifactRefs.evidence: Goal brief preserves adding more browser-offline project-related tools and designing a beautiful usable tool page.

artifactRefs.path: npm run build and browser DOM/log verification
artifactRefs.evidence: Build passed with exit code 0; production preview route rendered and console error list was empty.
ImageTools now exposes six offline browser tools and matching responsive styles.
Production preview DOM contained the image tools hero and tabs: format export, transparent trim, palette, compose sheet, stitch, pixel/scale.
